GitHub Actions Secrets Required for Release Workflows

This file documents which repository secrets are required and why for:
1) .github/workflows/ios-release.yml
2) .github/workflows/android-release.yml

Where to set them:
GitHub repository -> Settings -> Secrets and variables -> Actions -> New repository secret

==================================================
1) iOS workflow: .github/workflows/ios-release.yml
==================================================

Required secrets:

- IOS_P12_BASE64
  What it is:
  Base64 of the iOS signing certificate file (.p12) that contains an Apple Distribution certificate and private key.
  Why needed:
  The workflow decodes and imports this certificate into a temporary keychain so codesign can sign the app.
  If missing/wrong:
  Validation fails with no valid code-signing identity, and IPA signing/publish cannot proceed.

- IOS_P12_PASSWORD
  What it is:
  Password used when exporting the .p12 certificate.
  Why needed:
  Required by security import to unlock and import the p12.
  If missing/wrong:
  Certificate import fails and signing identity is unavailable.

- IOS_MOBILEPROVISION_BASE64
  What it is:
  Base64 of the provisioning profile (.mobileprovision) for the app bundle id.
  Why needed:
  The workflow decodes it, validates bundle id match, installs profile, and uses its name for CodesignProvision.
  If missing/wrong:
  Validation fails due to profile read/mismatch issues, and IPA publish fails.

- APPSTORE_USERNAME
  What it is:
  Apple ID username allowed to upload builds to App Store Connect.
  Why needed:
  Used by xcrun altool for credential validation and final IPA upload.
  If missing/wrong:
  App Store credential validation fails or upload step fails.

- APPSTORE_APP_PASSWORD
  What it is:
  App-specific password generated for that Apple ID.
  Why needed:
  Used by xcrun altool together with APPSTORE_USERNAME for non-interactive authentication.
  If missing/wrong:
  App Store credential validation fails or upload step fails.

Notes:
- ios-release.yml auto-resolves the code-sign identity from the imported certificate, so no IOS_CODESIGN_KEY secret is required.
- The workflow validates credentials first, then builds IPA, then uploads.

======================================================
2) Android workflow: .github/workflows/android-release.yml
======================================================

Required secrets:

- ANDROID_KEYSTORE
  What it is:
  Base64 of the Android release keystore file.
  Why needed:
  The workflow decodes it to release.keystore and passes its path to dotnet publish signing parameters.
  If missing/wrong:
  Keystore decode/signing fails; release artifacts cannot be signed.

- ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS
  What it is:
  Key alias inside the keystore used for signing.
  Why needed:
  Passed as AndroidSigningKeyAlias during dotnet publish.
  If missing/wrong:
  Signing fails because the key entry cannot be found.

- ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD
  What it is:
  Password for the key entry (alias) in the keystore.
  Why needed:
  Passed as AndroidSigningKeyPass during dotnet publish.
  If missing/wrong:
  Signing fails while unlocking the key.

- ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD
  What it is:
  Password for the keystore container itself.
  Why needed:
  Passed as AndroidSigningStorePass during dotnet publish.
  If missing/wrong:
  Signing fails while opening the keystore.

Notes:
- The workflow can build aab, apk, or both, but all release outputs require the same signing secrets.
- Artifact collection only keeps signed files (*-Signed.aab, *-Signed.apk).

Quick check before running workflows:
- iOS: p12 includes Apple Distribution cert+private key, profile matches bundle id, App Store credentials are valid.
- Android: keystore base64 is complete, alias exists in keystore, key/store passwords are correct.
